title: trias rosea
preparations: Trias rosea has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. Trias rosea teas are often used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, while tinctures are commonly used to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Salves made from Trias rosea are typically used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Additionally, Trias rosea infusions and essential oils are also used for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties to support immune function and prevent infections.
side_effects: Trias rosea can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to headaches, dizziness, and nausea. In rare cases, improper use of trias rosea may also cause respiratory problems, such as bronchitis or asthma exacerbation. Additionally, interactions with other medications can increase the risk of side effects, making it essential to consult a healthcare professional before using the plant for health purposes.
